theme: design a lock rewiring immune to nudge attacks
title: Combination-Lock Rewiring Against Nudge Attacks
mutation: composite: the objective couples differential-spectrum-flattening (minimize the WORST-CASE shift/change collision count of a permutation of F_p) with monomial-map-theory (a permutation exponent d controls the difference polynomial's degree via gcd(d,p-1), hence bounds achievable flatness) and a hard derangement side-constraint (pi(x) != x), which forces affine dressing of the chosen monomial to stay feasible.
strategies: trivial: reproduce the checker's own reference construction (block-local Fisher-Yates shuffle with fixed seed, fixed points repaired by adjacent swap) -- reproduces the baseline exactly
greedy: one full-range random shuffle + repair (uniform random + repair, the canonical first attempt; no algebraic structure at all)

insight (strong): scan odd exponents d coprime to p-1, evaluate each candidate's peak leakage in O(p) via the shift-invariant substitution u=x/s (which reduces every shift's difference distribution to the single canonical polynomial (u+1)^d - u^d), keep the best d, then affine-dress it (pi(x) = a*x^d + c) searching (a,c) via a forbidden-value set to kill every fixed point without touching the peak leakage

half-insight trap: use the smallest valid exponent found by the gcd(d,p-1)=1 criterion without scanning further candidates -- often ties the true optimum, but on primes where p-1 has several small prime factors (3, 5, 7, ...) it lands on a distinctly worse exponent than a real scan would find
